data.title: Specsmith Must Govern Itself
data.description: Specsmith must govern its own governance layer and use it for all changes.

records.label: Governance Files Must Be Owned by Specsmith

data.title: Governance Files Must Be Owned by Specsmith
data.description: Only Specsmith may create, update, or delete the human‑readable governance files `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, `TESTS.md`, and `LEDGER.md`.

records.label: Machine State Must Reflect Governance State

data.title: Machine State Must Reflect Governance State
data.description: Every machine‑readable state file under `.specsmith/` must be derived from its corresponding human‑readable governance file and remain in sync.

records.label: Requirements Must Be Derived from Architecture

data.title: Requirements Must Be Derived from Architecture
data.description: Specsmith must parse `ARCHITECTURE.md` to produce initial requirements.

data.title: Requirement IDs Must Be Stable
data.description: Once assigned, a requirement ID must never change or be reused.

data.title: Preflight Validation Must Be Performed
data.description: Before any governance action, the system must validate inputs and produce structured output with required fields.

records.label: Test Cases Must Be Generated from Requirements

data.title: Test Cases Must Be Generated from Requirements
data.description: For each requirement, Specsmith must create or link a test case that can prove the requirement.

records.label: Each Requirement Must Link to At Least One Test

data.title: Each Requirement Must Link to At Least One Test
data.description: Each requirement must be traceable to at least one test case.

records.label: Work Items Must Be Created for Accepted Requirements

data.title: Work Items Must Be Created for Accepted Requirements
data.description: When a requirement is accepted, a unique work item must be created.

records.label: Requirements Must Include Priority and Status

data.title: Requirements Must Include Priority and Status
data.description: Each requirement record must contain `priority` and `status` attributes.

data.title: Verification Must Include Confidence Scoring
data.description: Every verification run must produce a numeric confidence score along with pass/fail.

records.label: Equilibrium Must Be Reached Before Finalizing

data.title: Equilibrium Must Be Reached Before Finalizing
data.description: A work item may be marked finished only when its verification confidence meets or exceeds the configured threshold and no contradictions remain.

data.title: Retry Recommendations Must Be Provided
data.description: Specsmith must output retry recommendations when verification fails.

data.title: Retries Must Be Bounded
data.description: Each retry mechanism may not exceed a fixed maximum number of attempts.

records.label: Every Governance Action Must Record a Ledger Event

data.title: Every Governance Action Must Record a Ledger Event
data.description: All changes are logged to `LEDGER.md` and `.specsmith/ledger.jsonl` with timestamp and type.

data.title: Trace Chain Must Be Tamper‑Evident
data.description: The trace chain must use chained cryptographic hashes to provide tamper evidence.

records.label: OpenCode Must Own Execution and Tools

data.title: OpenCode Must Own Execution and Tools
data.description: All filesystem operations and tool executions are performed by OpenCode, not Specsmith directly.

data.title: Specsmith Core Must Be Integration‑Agnostic
data.description: The core logic must run without dependency on any particular integration implementation.

data.title: Verification Must Evaluate Changed Files
data.description: Verification must analyze which files were changed and only evaluate affected test cases.

data.title: Verification Must Evaluate Diff Relevance
data.description: Verification must determine whether a diff impacts any requirement or test case and ignore irrelevant changes.

data.title: Verification Must Evaluate Test Results
data.description: Verification must compare actual output against expected and quantify failures.

records.label: Verification Must Evaluate Contradictions and Uncertainty

data.title: Verification Must Evaluate Contradictions and Uncertainty
data.description: Verification must identify logical contradictions and uncertainty metrics.

records.label: Requirement Schema Must Include Source Location, Type, Priority, Confidence, Status, and Timestamps

data.title: Requirement Schema Must Include Source Location, Type, Priority, Confidence, Status, and Timestamps
data.description: Each requirement record must contain these schema fields.

records.label: Test Case Model Must Include Required Fields

data.title: Test Case Model Must Include Required Fields
data.description: All test case records must contain all required fields.

records.label: Work Item Model Must Include Required Fields

data.title: Work Item Model Must Include Required Fields
data.description: Each work item record must contain required fields such as id, status, priority.

records.label: Preflight Output Schema Must Include Decision, Work Item ID, Priority, Requirement IDs, Test Case IDs, Confidence Target

data.title: Preflight Output Schema Must Include Decision, Work Item ID, Priority, Requirement IDs, Test Case IDs, Confidence Target
data.description: Structured preflight output must list these fields.

records.label: Verification Input Must Include Diffs, Tests, Logs, and Changed Files

data.title: Verification Input Must Include Diffs, Tests, Logs, and Changed Files
data.description: Verification input must contain file diffs, test results, execution logs, and list of changed files.

records.label: Retry Strategy Mapping Must Be Defined

data.title: Retry Strategy Mapping Must Be Defined
data.description: Retries map failures to strategies such as narrow_scope, expand_scope, fix_tests, rollback, and stop.

records.label: Integration Adapter Interface Must Provide Required Capabilities

data.title: Integration Adapter Interface Must Provide Required Capabilities
data.description: Specsmith must provide filesystem and shell execution functions via the integration adapter.

records.label: Specsmith CLI Commands Must Be Explicitly Defined

data.title: Specsmith CLI Commands Must Be Explicitly Defined
data.description: Specsmith CLI must expose commands such as preflight, verify, requirements list/show/accept/reject, tests generate/list, status, and ledger list.

records.label: Sequencing Rules Must Enforce Valid States

data.title: Sequencing Rules Must Enforce Valid States
data.description: Bootstrap and sequence transitions must follow the defined order.

data.title: Configuration Settings for Optional Features
data.description: Specsmith must read optional feature flags from .specsmith/config.yml.

data.title: Default Enablement of Optional Features
data.description: All optional Specsmith features must be enabled by default unless overridden.

data.title: Evidence ZIP Archive Generation
data.description: Specsmith must generate evidence ZIP archive for selected artifacts.

data.title: Evidence Manifest Generation
data.description: Manifest must list artifacts and metadata in evidence archive.

data.title: Per‑File SHA‑256 Hashing in Evidence
data.description: Every file in evidence archive must have a SHA‑256 hash.

data.title: Final Evidence ZIP SHA‑256 Hash
data.description: The final evidence ZIP archive must have a computed SHA‑256 hash.

data.title: Author/Owner Metadata Capture
data.description: Evidence archive must record author/owner information for each artifact.

data.title: Git Commit Metadata Inclusion
data.description: Evidence archive must incorporate current git commit hash when available.

data.title: Ledger Reference Inclusion
data.description: Evidence archive must reference relevant ledger entries for traceability.

data.title: Trusted Timestamp Token Support
data.description: Evidence archive may include an RFC 3161 trusted timestamp token if enabled.

data.title: Legal/IP Disclaimer Requirement
data.description: Specsmith must provide a disclaimer that evidence does not guarantee legal ownership.

data.title: Ledger Event Hash Chaining
data.description: Each ledger event must be hashed and chained to previous event for tamper evidence.

data.title: Ledger Event on Work Proposal
data.description: Specsmith must create ledger event when a work item is proposed.

data.title: Ledger Event on Work Completion
data.description: Specsmith must create ledger event upon completion of each work item or batch.

data.title: README.md Generation and Synchronization
data.description: Specsmith must generate README.md if missing and keep it synchronized.

data.title: CHANGELOG.md Generation and Synchronization
data.description: Specsmith must generate CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog and keep it updated.

data.title: Keep a Changelog Compliance
data.description: CHANGELOG.md must follow Keep a Changelog format.

data.title: Semantic Versioning Support
data.description: Specsmith must understand and support Semantic Versioning for releases.

data.title: Guided Version Bump Workflow
data.description: Specsmith must provide a guided workflow for bumping version numbers.

data.title: Guided Release Strategy Workflow
data.description: Specsmith must offer a guided workflow to determine release strategy.

data.title: Guided Branching Strategy Workflow
data.description: Specsmith must offer a guided workflow for selecting a branching strategy.

data.title: Default GitFlow Branching Model
data.description: Specsmith’s default branching model is GitFlow unless overridden.

data.title: Guided Branching Modification
data.description: Specsmith must allow guided modifications to the branching model.

data.title: GitHub License Generation
data.description: Specsmith must generate license files compatible with GitHub/choosealicense.

data.title: Commercial License Drafting Guidance
data.description: Specsmith must provide guidance for drafting commercial licenses, including disclaimer.

data.title: Local Git Commit After Work
data.description: Specsmith should commit local changes after each completed work item or batch.

data.title: Confidence Threshold Configuration
data.description: Specsmith must allow configuring epistemic confidence threshold via config file.

data.title: Iteration Continuation Until Threshold
data.description: Work iterations continue until epistemic confidence reaches threshold.

data.title: Indefinite Iteration Default
data.description: Specsmith defaults to indefinite iteration unless user sets limits.

data.title: Max Iterations Configuration
data.description: Specsmith must allow configuring maximum iterations.

data.title: Token/Cost/Time Limits Configuration
data.description: Specsmith must allow configuring token spend, session cost, and elapsed time limits.

data.title: Stop‑and‑Align Behavior
data.description: Specsmith must stop when confidence cannot improve and engage user for alignment.

data.title: Interactive Correction Workflow
data.description: After stopping, Specsmith should provide an interactive correction workflow.

records.label: Nexus Runtime Must Not Own Governance

data.title: Nexus Runtime Must Not Own Governance
data.description: The Nexus agent runtime must defer preflight, requirement mapping, verification, retry decisions, and ledger writing to Specsmith.

records.label: Nexus Must Provide Required Agent Roles

data.title: Nexus Must Provide Required Agent Roles
data.description: Nexus must instantiate PlannerAgent, ShellAgent, CodeAgent, ReviewerAgent, MemoryAgent, GitAgent, HumanProxyAgent, and an Executor node.

records.label: Nexus Tool Layer Must Expose Required Tools

data.title: Nexus Tool Layer Must Expose Required Tools
data.description: Nexus must expose run_shell, read_file, write_file, patch_file, list_files, grep, git_diff, git_status, run_tests, open_url, search_docs, and remember_project_fact.

records.label: Nexus Safety Middleware Must Block Unsafe Commands

data.title: Nexus Safety Middleware Must Block Unsafe Commands
data.description: The safety middleware must block or require explicit approval for unsafe shell patterns including rm -rf, git push, docker compose down -v, database migrations, deploy commands, and secret reads.

records.label: Nexus Tool Arguments Must Be JSON Validated

data.title: Nexus Tool Arguments Must Be JSON Validated
data.description: All Nexus tool calls must validate that arguments are JSON-serializable before execution.

data.title: Nexus Must Normalize File Paths
data.description: All file paths supplied to Nexus tools must be normalized to absolute, resolved paths before access.

data.title: Nexus Must Index the Repository
data.description: Nexus must populate .repo-index/ with files.json, tags, test_commands.json, architecture.md, and conventions.md as available.

records.label: Nexus REPL Must Support Slash Commands

data.title: Nexus REPL Must Support Slash Commands
data.description: The Nexus REPL must support /plan, /ask, /fix, /test, /commit, /pr, /undo, /context, /exit.

data.title: Nexus Output Contract
data.description: Each Nexus task response must include sections Plan, Commands to run, Files changed, Diff, Test results, and Next action.

data.title: vLLM Image Must Be Pinned
data.description: The Nexus docker-compose.yml must pin the vLLM image to a specific tag (vllm/vllm-openai:v0.8.5) and not use latest.

data.title: vLLM Must Serve l1-nexus Model
data.description: The Nexus docker-compose.yml must publish the served model as l1-nexus and use the Hermes tool-call parser.

records.label: Nexus Tool Executor Registration Must Be Unique

data.title: Nexus Tool Executor Registration Must Be Unique
data.description: Each Nexus tool must be registered with the AG2 executor exactly once; LLM-side tool signatures may be attached to multiple caller agents but the execution function must not be re-registered to avoid AG2 override warnings.

records.label: Safe Cleanup Must Default to Dry-Run

data.title: Safe Cleanup Must Default to Dry-Run
data.description: The Specsmith safe-cleanup capability must default to dry-run mode and only delete files when an explicit apply flag is provided.

records.label: Safe Cleanup Must Use a Hard-Coded Target List

data.title: Safe Cleanup Must Use a Hard-Coded Target List
data.description: Safe cleanup must only consider the canonical built-in target list and must reject user-supplied arbitrary paths.

records.label: Safe Cleanup Must Protect Governance and Source

data.title: Safe Cleanup Must Protect Governance and Source
data.description: Safe cleanup must refuse to delete .git, .specsmith, governance markdown files, pyproject.toml, README.md, LICENSE, CHANGELOG.md, src/, tests/, docs/, scripts/, .repo-index/, .github/, .vscode/, third-party agent integration directories (such as .agents/), and project configuration dotfiles.

records.label: Safe Cleanup Must Emit a Structured Report

data.title: Safe Cleanup Must Emit a Structured Report
data.description: Safe cleanup must return a report containing the lists of removed paths, skipped paths with reasons, and total bytes reclaimed, suitable for inclusion as ledger evidence.

records.label: Safe Cleanup Must Be Exposed via Specsmith CLI

data.title: Safe Cleanup Must Be Exposed via Specsmith CLI
data.description: The Specsmith CLI must expose the safe cleanup capability as `specsmith clean`, supporting `--apply`, `--json`, and `--project-dir`. When `--apply` is used and `LEDGER.md` exists, the run must be recorded as a `cleanup` ledger event tagged with REQ-077..REQ-080.

records.label: CLI Console Must Be UTF-8 Safe Across Platforms

data.title: CLI Console Must Be UTF-8 Safe Across Platforms
data.description: All Specsmith CLI output (rich Console) must render UTF-8 glyphs (such as warning, arrow, check, cross) without raising UnicodeEncodeError on Windows code pages such as cp1252. The console factory must reconfigure stdout/stderr to UTF-8 and disable rich's legacy_windows renderer.

records.label: Canonical Test Specification File Is TESTS.md

data.title: Canonical Test Specification File Is TESTS.md
data.description: The canonical test specification file is named `TESTS.md` (replacing the legacy names `TESTS.md`, `TEST-SPEC.md`, and `TEST-SPECS.md`). Specsmith code, governance documents, templates, scaffolder output, importer overlay, auditor checks, retrieval index, exporter, validator, REPL skill files, ReadTheDocs site, and CLI help must all reference `TESTS.md`. Legacy filenames must not be created by new scaffolds, must be auto-renamed by `specsmith migrate-project`, and must not be referenced in user-facing docs.

data.title: Natural-Language Governance Broker
data.description: Specsmith must expose a Nexus broker module (`specsmith.agent.broker`) that translates plain-language user utterances into Specsmith-governed work without the user reasoning about REQ IDs, TEST IDs, or work items. The broker must classify intent (read-only ask vs change vs release), infer affected scope from the local `.repo-index` and existing requirements, invoke `specsmith preflight` and `specsmith verify` as the only sources of governance decisions, render plain-language plans and outcomes, hide REQ/TEST/work-item IDs by default (revealed only on `/why`, `/show-governance`, or `--verbose`), bound retries per REQ-014, escalate to a single user clarification on stop-and-align (REQ-063), and never invent governance content (REQ/TEST drafting requires explicit user confirmation).

data.title: specsmith preflight CLI Subcommand
data.description: The Specsmith CLI must expose a `specsmith preflight <utterance>` subcommand that reads `REQUIREMENTS.md` and `.specsmith/` state, classifies intent and infers scope, and emits a JSON object with at least the keys `decision` (one of `accepted`, `needs_clarification`, `blocked`, `rejected`), `work_item_id`, `requirement_ids`, `test_case_ids`, `confidence_target`, and `instruction`. Read-only asks accept by default, destructive intents require clarification, and changes with no matching scope return `needs_clarification` with a one-sentence question. The CLI must support `--project-dir`, `--json`, and `--verbose`.

records.label: Nexus REPL Must Gate Execution on Preflight Acceptance

data.title: Nexus REPL Must Gate Execution on Preflight Acceptance
data.description: When a non-slash utterance flows through the broker, the Nexus REPL must only invoke the AG2 orchestrator's `run_task` if the preflight decision is `accepted`. For any other decision (`needs_clarification`, `blocked`, `rejected`), the REPL must print the broker's plain-language clarification or rejection and return to the prompt without executing.

records.label: Nexus REPL Must Drive Execution Through the Bounded-Retry Harness

data.title: Nexus REPL Must Drive Execution Through the Bounded-Retry Harness
data.description: When the preflight decision is `accepted`, the Nexus REPL must drive the AG2 orchestrator through `specsmith.agent.broker.execute_with_governance`, supplying an executor that wraps `orchestrator.run_task` and synthesizes a result dict (`equilibrium`, `confidence`, `summary`). The harness must honor `DEFAULT_RETRY_BUDGET` (REQ-014), surface the single clarifying question on stop-and-align (REQ-063), and never call `run_task` directly outside the harness.

records.label: specsmith preflight Must Resolve Test Case IDs From Machine State

data.title: specsmith preflight Must Resolve Test Case IDs From Machine State
data.description: The `specsmith preflight` CLI must populate `test_case_ids` in its JSON payload by joining the matched `requirement_ids` against `.specsmith/testcases.json` (or `TESTS.md` when the JSON is unavailable). When the resolved set is non-empty the CLI must include every matching `TEST-NNN` id and must never invent ids not present in machine state.

data.title: Nexus Live l1-nexus Smoke Test
data.description: Specsmith must ship a `scripts/nexus_smoke.py` script that POSTs a minimal chat-completions request to a running vLLM `l1-nexus` container at `http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions` and reports whether the model responded with a well-formed `choices[0].message.content`. A pytest integration test must invoke the script and skip unless the environment variable `NEXUS_LIVE=1` is set, so the suite stays green offline but is verifiable when the container is up.

records.label: Nexus Documentation Must Describe Broker, Preflight, and Gated Execution

data.title: Nexus Documentation Must Describe Broker, Preflight, and Gated Execution
data.description: `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `README.md`, and `docs/` must describe the natural-language broker (REQ-084), the `specsmith preflight` CLI (REQ-085), the REPL execution gate (REQ-086), and the bounded-retry harness (REQ-087), including the `/why` toggle and an end-to-end example flow. Documentation must not surface REQ/TEST/WI tokens to the user except inside the explicit `/why` block.

records.label: Orchestrator Must Return a Structured TaskResult

data.title: Orchestrator Must Return a Structured TaskResult
data.description: `orchestrator.run_task` must return a `TaskResult` dataclass with at least the fields `equilibrium: bool`, `confidence: float`, `summary: str`, `files_changed: list[str]`, and `test_results: dict`. The Nexus REPL's broker branch must consume this dataclass directly when feeding `execute_with_governance` (REQ-087); the broker must not synthesize `equilibrium` from a boolean cast of the summary string.

records.label: specsmith preflight CLI Must Use Decision-Specific Exit Codes

data.title: specsmith preflight CLI Must Use Decision-Specific Exit Codes
data.description: The `specsmith preflight` CLI must exit `0` for `accepted`, `2` for `needs_clarification`, and `3` for `blocked` or `rejected` decisions, so CI pipelines and shell wrappers can branch on intent without parsing the JSON payload. The JSON payload must continue to print on stdout for both success and non-zero exits.

records.label: Accepted preflight Must Record a Ledger Event

data.title: Accepted preflight Must Record a Ledger Event
data.description: When `specsmith preflight` produces an `accepted` decision and `LEDGER.md` exists in the project root, the CLI must append a `preflight` ledger event tagged with `REQ-085` plus the resolved `requirement_ids`. The event must record the utterance, the assigned `work_item_id`, and the `confidence_target`, so every accepted preflight is traceable end-to-end.

records.label: /why Must Surface Post-Run Governance in the REPL

data.title: /why Must Surface Post-Run Governance in the REPL
data.description: When `verbose_governance` is on (toggled by `/why` or `/show-governance`), after the REPL drives `execute_with_governance` for an accepted utterance it must print a single `[/why]` block summarizing the assigned `work_item_id`, the matched `requirement_ids` and `test_case_ids`, the post-run confidence, and whether the bounded-retry harness reached equilibrium. When verbose mode is off, the post-run governance block must not be emitted.

records.label: Nexus Live Smoke Run Must Be Reproducible Evidence

data.title: Nexus Live Smoke Run Must Be Reproducible Evidence
data.description: A live or honestly-skipped invocation of `scripts/nexus_smoke.py` must be captured under `.specsmith/runs/WI-NEXUS-011/logs.txt` so the project ledger preserves at least one reproducible record of the broker -> preflight -> orchestrator -> vLLM end-to-end path (or a documented reason the live container could not be reached in the current environment).

records.label: Bounded-Retry Harness Must Map Failures to Retry Strategies

data.title: Bounded-Retry Harness Must Map Failures to Retry Strategies
data.description: When `execute_with_governance` exhausts its retry budget (REQ-014), it must classify the last executor report against the canonical retry strategy mapping (REQ-028): `narrow_scope`, `expand_scope`, `fix_tests`, `rollback`, or `stop`. The classification must be exposed on `RunResult.strategy` and surfaced in the clarifying question (REQ-063) so the user gets one concrete next-action label rather than only a free-form sentence.

data.title: specsmith verify CLI Subcommand
data.description: The Specsmith CLI must expose a `specsmith verify` subcommand that consumes the verification input contract (REQ-027): file diffs, test results, execution logs, and changed files (paths or `--stdin` JSON). The subcommand must emit a JSON object with at least `equilibrium`, `confidence`, `summary`, `files_changed`, `test_results`, and `retry_strategy`. Exit code 0 on equilibrium with confidence ≥ the configured threshold, 2 when retry is recommended, and 3 when stop-and-align is required.

records.label: Confidence Threshold Must Be Read From .specsmith/config.yml

data.title: Confidence Threshold Must Be Read From .specsmith/config.yml
data.description: Both `specsmith preflight` and the broker's `run_preflight` helper must consult `.specsmith/config.yml` for the `epistemic.confidence_threshold` value (REQ-058) and use it as the floor for the JSON `confidence_target` field whenever it is greater than the heuristic default. When the config file is absent or unparseable, the existing heuristic defaults must continue to apply.

records.label: Accepted Preflight Must Record a Distinct work_proposal Event

data.title: Accepted Preflight Must Record a Distinct work_proposal Event
data.description: When `specsmith preflight` produces an `accepted` decision and assigns a brand-new `work_item_id`, the CLI must append a `work_proposal` ledger event in addition to the existing `preflight` event (REQ-044). The `work_proposal` entry must reference REQ-044 and REQ-085, include the `work_item_id` and matched `requirement_ids`, and must NOT be emitted when the underlying `work_item_id` already appears in `LEDGER.md` (no duplicate proposals).

records.label: Broker Scope Inference May Surface Stress-Test Critical Failures

data.title: Broker Scope Inference May Surface Stress-Test Critical Failures
data.description: When the user passes `--stress` to `specsmith preflight` and the matched requirements set is non-empty, the CLI must invoke the existing AEE `StressTester` against those belief artifacts and surface any critical failures in the JSON payload as a `stress_warnings` list. The narration (verbose mode) must include a one-sentence plain-English warning when at least one critical failure is found. The flag must default off so unrelated tests continue to pass.

data.title: Lint Baseline Must Be Clean
data.description: `ruff check src/ tests/` and `ruff format --check src/ tests/` must both exit zero on `develop`. The lint job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` enforces this contract. Per-file ignores in `pyproject.toml` are reserved for documentation modules whose long lines are intentional (e.g. `toolrules.py`, `tool_installer.py`).

data.title: Type-Check Baseline Must Be Clean
data.description: `mypy src/specsmith/` must exit zero on `develop`. Strict-mypy is preserved for the historically-typed modules; dynamically-typed modules in `specsmith.agent.*`, `specsmith.console_utils`, `specsmith.serve`, and the agent-orchestrator surface are explicitly enumerated in the `[[tool.mypy.overrides]]` `ignore_errors=true` block of `pyproject.toml` until they are individually annotated.

records.label: Security Baseline Tolerates Unfixed pip Advisory

data.title: Security Baseline Tolerates Unfixed pip Advisory
data.description: The CI security job must upgrade pip to the latest release before invoking `pip-audit`, and must pass the `--ignore-vuln CVE-2026-3219` flag for the unfixed pip advisory so the runner's own pip version does not block PRs. Specsmith's actual runtime dependencies (click, jinja2, pyyaml, pydantic, rich) must remain pip-audit clean; any new advisory against them must trigger a dependency bump rather than another ignore-flag.

records.label: Work Items Must Mirror Implemented REQs

data.title: Work Items Must Mirror Implemented REQs
data.description: `.specsmith/workitems.json` must derive from `.specsmith/requirements.json` and `.specsmith/testcases.json`. For each REQ-N there must be a matching WORK-N entry with `requirement_id=REQ-N`, `test_case_ids` listing every TEST joined by `requirement_id`, and `status=complete` when the REQ is implemented in source. The `scripts/sync_workitems.py` helper is the canonical sync.

records.label: Live Smoke Evidence Must Be Reproducible Or Honestly Skipped

data.title: Live Smoke Evidence Must Be Reproducible Or Honestly Skipped
data.description: A live or honestly-skipped invocation of `scripts/nexus_smoke.py` against the configured `l1-nexus` model must be captured under `.specsmith/runs/WI-NEXUS-011/logs.txt`. The skip note must include a fresh probe attempt, a timestamp, and the hardware/environment reason the live container could not be reached.

data.title: Kairos Must Surface Governance Commands
data.description: The Kairos terminal client must provide UI access to the three primary governance operations: preflight gate, verify, and governance trace (`/why`). These are surfaced via the Governance settings page and the BYOE proxy at `http://127.0.0.1:7700`. *Note: the legacy `specsmith-vscode` commands (`specsmith.runPreflight`, `specsmith.runVerify`, `specsmith.toggleWhy`) are deprecated; Kairos is the flagship client as of v0.10.1.*

data.title: ARCHITECTURE.md Must Reflect Current State
data.description: `ARCHITECTURE.md` must contain a 'Current State' section listing the realized broker, harness, retry strategies, CI baseline, Kairos governance integration, live-smoke evidence note, and documentation surface. The section is the source of truth for 'the system as built' and must be updated each time a release is cut.

records.label: Real Verifier Signal Must Drive Confidence

data.title: Real Verifier Signal Must Drive Confidence
data.description: `Orchestrator._build_task_result` must derive `TaskResult.confidence` and `equilibrium` from a real verifier (`src/specsmith/agent/verifier.py`) that inspects test results, ruff output, and mypy output for the changed files. The hardcoded 0.85 / 0.4 / 0.0 placeholder must be removed.

records.label: Live `l1-nexus` Smoke Overlay Must Produce ok=true on 7B Hardware

data.title: Live `l1-nexus` Smoke Overlay Must Produce ok=true on 7B Hardware
data.description: Specsmith ships a `docker-compose.smoke.yml` overlay that swaps `l1-nexus` to a 7B GPTQ-Int4 model fitting <=8 GB VRAM, and `.specsmith/runs/WI-NEXUS-029/logs.txt` documents how to capture an `ok: true` smoke result with `NEXUS_LIVE=1` against that overlay.

records.label: End-to-End Nexus Path Must Be Integration-Tested

data.title: End-to-End Nexus Path Must Be Integration-Tested
data.description: `tests/test_e2e_nexus.py` exercises the broker -> preflight -> harness -> orchestrator -> verifier path with a `FakeOrchestrator` and asserts ledger events, `RunResult.success`, and retry-strategy classification on a scripted failure-then-recovery sequence.

records.label: Mypy Strict Carveout Must Shrink Toward Zero

data.title: Mypy Strict Carveout Must Shrink Toward Zero
data.description: At least the four newly-annotated dynamic agent modules (`broker`, `safety`, `console_utils`, `indexer`) are fully type-annotated and removed from the `[[tool.mypy.overrides]] ignore_errors=true` block in `pyproject.toml`. The remaining carveout (orchestrator, repl, tools, cleanup, serve) is documented as a 1.x cleanup target.

records.label: Streaming Token Bridge Must Emit JSONL Events

data.title: Streaming Token Bridge Must Emit JSONL Events
data.description: A new `specsmith chat <utterance> --json-events` CLI subcommand drives the broker + harness end-to-end and emits a JSONL event stream on stdout with at least the event types `block_start`, `token`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`, `block_complete`, and `task_complete`. Each event is a single JSON object on its own line.

data.title: Block-Based Output Schema
data.description: Every `block_start` event carries a `block_id`, `kind` (one of `plan`, `message`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`, `diff`, `test_results`, `verdict`), `agent`, and `timestamp`. The corresponding `block_complete` reuses the same `block_id`. Schema is documented in `docs/site/chat-events.md`.

data.title: Plan Block Must Surface Steps
data.description: When the broker classifies an utterance as a `change` and preflight is `accepted`, the chat stream must emit a `plan` block whose payload is a list of `{step_id, title, status}` items. Status transitions (`pending` -> `running` -> `done` / `failed`) are emitted as `plan_step` events keyed by `step_id`.

records.label: Permission/Autonomy Tier Must Be Honored End-to-End

data.title: Permission/Autonomy Tier Must Be Honored End-to-End
data.description: `specsmith chat` accepts `--profile {safe,standard,open,admin}` (default reads `scaffold.yml`). Under `safe`, every tool call emits a `tool_request` event and waits for an inbound `tool_decision` line on stdin (`{decision: 'approve'|'deny'}`). Under `standard` / `open` the harness proceeds without prompting. The selected profile is recorded in the ledger entry.

records.label: Inline Diff Review Must Round-Trip Comments

data.title: Inline Diff Review Must Round-Trip Comments
data.description: `specsmith chat` emits a `diff` block per file changed by the orchestrator; subsequent stdin lines of the form `{type: 'comment', block_id, path, line, body}` are stored in the session memory and surfaced to the bounded-retry harness as additional context on the next attempt. `--comment` flag on `specsmith verify` does the equivalent for non-streaming use.

records.label: Predict-Only Preflight Must Not Allocate a Work Item

data.title: Predict-Only Preflight Must Not Allocate a Work Item
data.description: `specsmith preflight <utterance> --predict-only --json` returns the same JSON shape as the canonical `preflight` (intent, requirement_ids, instruction, etc.) but with `work_item_id == ''`, no ledger event written, and a new `predicted_refinement` field that suggests a tightened utterance. Used by IDE autocomplete.

records.label: Kairos Must Surface specsmith chat Stream

data.title: Kairos Must Surface specsmith chat Stream
data.description: The Kairos governance proxy (`/v1/chat/completions`) consumes the `specsmith chat --json-events` JSONL stream and exposes it to the agent session. *The deprecated `specsmith-vscode` `specsmith.openChat` command served this purpose for the VS Code extension; it has been superseded by the Kairos BYOE proxy.*

records.label: Project Rules Must Auto-Inject Into the System Prompt

data.title: Project Rules Must Auto-Inject Into the System Prompt
data.description: `src/specsmith/agent/rules.py:load_rules(project_dir)` reads `docs/governance/*_RULES.md` and the H-rules from `AGENTS.md`, returning a single deterministic system-prompt prefix string. The orchestrator prepends this string to every AG2 agent's `system_message` at construction time.

records.label: Persistent Session Memory Must Be Token-Budgeted

data.title: Persistent Session Memory Must Be Token-Budgeted
data.description: `src/specsmith/agent/memory.py` provides `append_turn(session_id, turn)` and `recent_turns(session_id, max_chars)` that read/write `.specsmith/sessions/<session_id>/turns.jsonl`. `specsmith chat --session-id <id>` injects the most recent N turns (within `max_chars`) into the orchestrator's first message.

records.label: MCP Tool Consumption Must Be Configuration-Driven

data.title: MCP Tool Consumption Must Be Configuration-Driven
data.description: `src/specsmith/agent/mcp.py:load_mcp_tools(project_dir)` reads `.specsmith/mcp.yml` (a list of `{name, command, args, env}` entries) and returns Nexus-tool wrappers that proxy to each external MCP server via stdio. The Specsmith safety middleware wraps every MCP tool call.

records.label: Dynamic Agent/Model Routing Must Be Pluggable

data.title: Dynamic Agent/Model Routing Must Be Pluggable
data.description: `src/specsmith/agent/router.py:choose_tier(intent, scope, retry_count)` returns one of `{coder, heavy, fast}` based on `.specsmith/config.yml routing:` overrides. The orchestrator builds a model-config map keyed by tier and selects the appropriate `llm_config` per agent.

data.title: Notebook Capture and Replay
data.description: `specsmith notebook record --session-id <id> --slug <name>` writes `docs/notebooks/<slug>.md` with the captured turns; `specsmith notebook replay <slug>` re-runs each utterance through `specsmith chat` (using the recorded `--profile`), re-checking governance gates.

records.label: Performance Baseline Must Be Measured and Tracked

data.title: Performance Baseline Must Be Measured and Tracked
data.description: `scripts/perf_smoke.py` synthesizes a 1000-REQ `REQUIREMENTS.md` in tmp_path, runs `specsmith preflight` 50 times, and writes p50 / p95 / p99 to `.specsmith/perf/baseline.json`. CI reports the deltas vs the committed baseline as a non-blocking warning.

data.title: Multi-Session Parallel Agents
data.description: `specsmith chat` accepts `--parent-session <id>`. When set, the spawned session's `task_complete` event also writes a `sub_session_complete` event into the parent's session log so the parent's plan-block can surface child outcomes.

data.title: Onboarding Path Must Be Verified
data.description: `specsmith doctor --onboarding` prints a checklist (CLI installed, env activated, scaffold.yml present, REQUIREMENTS.md present, vLLM endpoint reachable, ledger present) and exits non-zero if any required item is missing. `docs/site/getting-started.md` walks a fresh user from install to first accepted preflight.

data.title: Cross-Repo Security Sweep
data.description: The specsmith and kairos repos both run `pip-audit` / `cargo audit` in CI and fail on high-or-critical findings. Dependabot manifests in both repos are reviewed and any open alert at 1.0 release time is documented. *Note: the legacy `specsmith-vscode` npm audit requirement has been retired alongside the extension deprecation.*

data.title: 1.0 API Stability Commitment
data.description: `docs/site/api-stability.md` enumerates the public surfaces frozen at 1.0 (CLI subcommands and exit codes, JSON payload schemas for preflight / verify / chat events, broker module API, ledger event schemas, VS Code extension command IDs). The PyPI classifier is bumped to `Development Status :: 5 - Production/Stable` and `pyproject.toml` to `1.0.0`.

data.title: Typed ProjectOperations Layer
data.description: All tool handlers MUST use a typed `ProjectOperations` class for file, git/VCS, and search operations. Direct raw shell string assembly in tool handlers is prohibited.

data.title: ProjectOperations File Operations via pathlib
data.description: `ProjectOperations` MUST expose file operations (`read_file`, `write_file`, `list_dir`, `glob`, `search`) implemented via Python `pathlib`/`stdlib` — no subprocess calls.

data.title: ProjectOperations Git/VCS Operations
data.description: `ProjectOperations` MUST expose git/VCS operations (`status`, `log`, `diff`, `add`, `commit`, `push`, `create_branch`, `create_pr`) returning structured result objects.

data.title: ProjectOperations Typed Result Objects
data.description: All `ProjectOperations` methods MUST return a typed result containing at minimum `exit_code`, `stdout`, `stderr`, and `elapsed_ms`.

records.label: executor.py run_tracked Preserved as Narrow Fallback

data.title: executor.py run_tracked Preserved as Narrow Fallback
data.description: The existing `executor.py` `run_tracked()` function MUST be preserved as a narrow fallback for commands that have no Python equivalent.

data.title: ProjectOperations Cross-Platform
data.description: `ProjectOperations` MUST be cross-platform (Windows, Linux, macOS) without platform-specific code branches in call sites.

data.title: Harness Slash Commands Package
data.description: The `commands/` package MUST implement all priority harness slash commands available inside `specsmith run`.

data.title: Session Management Slash Commands
data.description: Session management commands MUST include: `/model`, `/provider`, `/tier`, `/status`, `/save`, `/clear`, `/compact`, `/export`.

data.title: Multi-Agent Slash Commands
data.description: Multi-agent commands MUST include: `/spawn`, `/team`, `/team-status`, `/worktree`.

data.title: Continuous Learning Slash Commands
data.description: Continuous learning commands MUST include: `/learn`, `/learn-eval`, `/instinct-status`, `/instinct-import`, `/instinct-export`.

data.title: Evaluation Slash Commands
data.description: Evaluation commands MUST include: `/eval define`, `/eval run`, `/eval report`, `/eval compare`.

data.title: Orchestration Slash Commands
data.description: Orchestration commands MUST include: `/multi-plan`, `/multi-execute`, `/route`.

data.title: Hook Control Slash Commands
data.description: Hook control commands MUST include: `/hooks-enable`, `/hooks-disable`, `/hook-profile`.

data.title: MCP Slash Commands
data.description: MCP commands MUST include: `/mcp-list`, `/mcp-add`, `/mcp-configure`.

data.title: Security Slash Commands
data.description: Security commands MUST include: `/security-scan`, `/audit-prompt`.

data.title: AgentTool for Subagent Spawning
data.description: The runner MUST provide an `AgentTool` (TaskTool) as a native LLM-callable tool that spawns subagent instances.

data.title: Hub-and-Spoke and Agent-Teams Coordination
data.description: Subagent spawning MUST support hub-and-spoke and agent-teams (peer-to-peer via filesystem mailbox) coordination modes.

data.title: Filesystem Mailbox for Agent Teams
data.description: The filesystem mailbox for agent teams MUST be stored at `.specsmith/teams/{team}/mailbox/{agent}.json`.

data.title: Git Worktree Isolation for Subagents
data.description: When `isolation=worktree`, the spawner MUST create a git worktree at `.specsmith/worktrees/{agent_id}/`.

data.title: No Recursive Subagent Nesting
data.description: Subagents MUST NOT be able to spawn further subagents (no recursive nesting).

data.title: Distilled Summary from Subagents
data.description: The parent agent MUST receive a distilled summary from each subagent on completion, not the full transcript.

data.title: Agent Teams Feature Flag Gated
data.description: Agent team mode MUST be gated behind a feature flag (`SPECSMITH_AGENT_TEAMS=1`).

data.title: Orchestrator Meta-Agent for Routing
data.description: specsmith MUST provide an orchestrator meta-agent for task classification, routing, and optimization — not execution.

data.title: Orchestrator Defaults to Local Ollama
data.description: The orchestrator MUST default to a small local Ollama model so orchestration incurs zero cloud API cost.

data.title: Agent Registry with Capability Metadata
data.description: The orchestrator MUST maintain an agent registry with type, model, provider, cost_tier, capabilities, avg_latency_ms, confidence.

data.title: Orchestrator Emits One Structured Next-Action
data.description: The orchestrator MUST emit exactly one structured next-action per task.

data.title: Cost-Aware Routing
data.description: The orchestrator MUST route cheap tasks to Ollama workers and complex tasks to cloud providers.

data.title: Post-Session Self-Evaluation for Routing Thresholds
data.description: The orchestrator MUST run a post-session self-evaluation to update routing thresholds.

records.label: Feature Flag System for Tool Schema Visibility

data.title: Feature Flag System for Tool Schema Visibility
data.description: specsmith MUST implement a feature-flag system controlling which tool schemas are sent to the LLM.

records.label: Feature Flags via Environment and scaffold.yml

data.title: Feature Flags via Environment and scaffold.yml
data.description: Feature flags MUST be configurable via environment variables and `scaffold.yml` under `agent.flags`.

records.label: Agent Teams and Advanced Features Flag-Gated

data.title: Agent Teams and Advanced Features Flag-Gated
data.description: Agent teams, worktree isolation, KAIROS daemon mode, security scanner, and MCP tools MUST be flag-gated.

data.title: Instinct Persistence System
data.description: specsmith MUST implement an instinct persistence system in `src/specsmith/instinct.py`.

data.title: Instinct Record Schema
data.description: Each instinct record MUST contain: id, trigger_pattern, content, confidence, project_scope, created, last_used, use_count.

data.title: SESSION_END Hook Extracts Candidate Instincts
data.description: The `SESSION_END` hook MUST extract candidate instincts for user review.

data.title: /learn Command Promotes Pattern to Instinct
data.description: The `/learn` command MUST promote a pattern to an instinct with an initial confidence score.

data.title: Instinct Confidence Updated on Application
data.description: Instinct confidence MUST be updated based on application success/rejection.

records.label: Instincts Importable and Exportable as Markdown

data.title: Instincts Importable and Exportable as Markdown
data.description: Instincts MUST be importable and exportable as `.md` files.

data.title: /instinct-status Displays Active Instincts
data.description: `/instinct-status` MUST display all active instincts sorted by confidence.

data.title: Eval Harness Module
data.description: specsmith MUST implement an eval harness in `src/specsmith/eval/`.

data.title: Eval Data Model
data.description: The eval model MUST define: Task, Trial, Grader, Transcript, Outcome.

data.title: Eval Tasks Stored as Markdown
data.description: Tasks MUST be stored as Markdown at `.specsmith/evals/{feature}.md` with YAML frontmatter.

data.title: Three Grader Types
data.description: The harness MUST support CodeGrader, ModelGrader, and HumanFlag grader types.

data.title: pass@k and pass^k Metrics
data.description: The harness MUST compute `pass@k` and `pass^k` metrics.

data.title: Git-Based Outcome Grading by Default
data.description: Default grading MUST be git-based outcome grading, not execution-path assertion.

data.title: /eval run --trials k
data.description: `/eval run --trials k` MUST run k independent trials and report results.

data.title: Capability vs Regression Eval Distinction
data.description: The harness MUST distinguish capability evals from regression evals.

data.title: Cross-Session Agent Memory
data.description: specsmith MUST implement cross-session agent memory in `src/specsmith/memory.py`.

data.title: Agent Memory Structured JSON
data.description: Agent memory MUST be structured JSON with accumulated patterns, preferred approaches, known project facts, and failure history.

records.label: SESSION_START Hook Injects Memories into System Prompt

data.title: SESSION_START Hook Injects Memories into System Prompt
data.description: The `SESSION_START` hook MUST inject relevant memories into the system prompt (token-budget-aware).

records.label: Agent Memory Compatible with Theia AI Convention

data.title: Agent Memory Compatible with Theia AI Convention
data.description: Agent memory layout MUST be compatible with Theia AI's `~/.theia/agent-memory/` convention.

data.title: Runtime Hook Enable/Disable
data.description: Hooks MUST be enable/disable-able at runtime without restarting the session.

data.title: Hook Profiles via /hook-profile
data.description: Hook profiles MUST be loadable via `/hook-profile`.

data.title: New Hook Trigger Events
data.description: New triggers: `SUBAGENT_START`, `SUBAGENT_STOP`, `CONTEXT_COMPACT`, `EVAL_PASS`, `EVAL_FAIL`.

data.title: SUBAGENT_START Hook Can Block Spawn
data.description: `SUBAGENT_START` MUST fire before spawning; a hook MAY block the spawn.

data.title: SUBAGENT_STOP Hook on Completion
data.description: `SUBAGENT_STOP` MUST fire when a subagent completes.

data.title: CONTEXT_COMPACT Hook Before Trimming
data.description: `CONTEXT_COMPACT` MUST fire before context trimming.

data.title: specsmith serve Command
data.description: specsmith MUST provide a `specsmith serve` command (already shipped in v0.7.0).

records.label: REST Endpoints for Session and Agent Management

data.title: REST Endpoints for Session and Agent Management
data.description: REST endpoints: `GET/POST /sessions`, `GET /agents`, `GET /instincts`, `GET /evals`, `POST /index`, `GET /health`.

records.label: WebSocket Endpoint for Live Session I/O

data.title: WebSocket Endpoint for Live Session I/O
data.description: WebSocket endpoint at `/ws/session/{id}` for live session I/O using the existing JSONL event schema.

records.label: EventSink Protocol for Stdout and WebSocket

data.title: EventSink Protocol for Stdout and WebSocket
data.description: `AgentRunner._emit_event()` MUST use an `EventSink` protocol (`StdoutSink` / `WebSocketSink`).

data.title: Kairos Terminal Connects via HTTP/WebSocket
data.description: The Kairos terminal MUST connect to `specsmith serve` over HTTP/WebSocket for all governance operations.

data.title: BM25 Retrieval Ranking
data.description: `retrieval.py` MUST be upgraded from term-frequency to BM25 ranking using `rank_bm25`.

data.title: File-Watcher Based Index Refresh
data.description: The retrieval index MUST support file-watcher-based refresh.

data.title: Token-Counted Retrieval Results
data.description: Retrieval results MUST be token-counted before injection to prevent context budget overruns.

data.title: MCP Server Configuration Templates
data.description: specsmith MUST provide MCP server configuration templates via `/mcp-add` or `specsmith mcp add`.

data.title: MCP Server Registry with Status
data.description: The MCP server registry MUST list configured servers with status and tool surfaces.

data.title: MCP Configuration in scaffold.yml
data.description: MCP configuration MUST be storable in `scaffold.yml` under `agent.mcp_servers`.

data.title: /security-scan Command
data.description: specsmith MUST provide a `/security-scan` command running a dedicated security analysis agent.

data.title: Security Scan Coverage
data.description: The security scan MUST check dependency vulnerabilities, OWASP-style code patterns, and exposed secrets.

data.title: /audit-prompt for Injection Analysis
data.description: `/audit-prompt` MUST analyze a prompt string for injection vectors.

data.title: Security Scan Results Stored Structurally
data.description: Security scan results MUST be structured and stored at `.specsmith/security-reports/`.

data.title: specsmith-ide Theia Application
data.description: A `specsmith-ide` application MUST be created on Eclipse Theia with `@theia/ai-core`, `@theia/ai-chat`, `@theia/ai-ide`.

data.title: specsmith-ide Extension Packages
data.description: specsmith-ide MUST ship: `@specsmith/ai-agents`, `@specsmith/epistemic-ui`, `@specsmith/eval-ui`, `@specsmith/service-client`.

records.label: specsmith-ide WebSocket Connection to specsmith serve

data.title: specsmith-ide WebSocket Connection to specsmith serve
data.description: specsmith-ide MUST connect to `specsmith serve` over WebSocket.

records.label: specsmith-ide Leverages Theia AI Native Tooling

data.title: specsmith-ide Leverages Theia AI Native Tooling
data.description: specsmith-ide MUST leverage Theia AI's existing MCP support, ShellExecutionTool, and agent skills system.

data.title: specsmith-ide Electron Desktop Packaging
data.description: specsmith-ide MUST be packageable as an Electron desktop application.

data.title: Tamper-Evident Agent Action Log
data.description: specsmith MUST maintain a tamper-evident, append-only agent action log capturing every governance decision, tool invocation, input, and output. The log chain MUST use SHA-256 chaining so any modification is detectable. Satisfies EU AI Act Art. 12 (logging obligations) and NIST AI RMF (GO-6).

data.title: Explanation Artifacts for Governance Decisions
data.description: Every governance decision (preflight, verify, classify) MUST produce an 'explanation artifact' recording: what data was used, which requirements were matched, why the decision was reached, and the confidence score. Satisfies EU AI Act Art. 13 (transparency), CFPB adverse-action requirements.

data.title: Action Log Replay and Export
data.description: specsmith MUST support structured replay and export of agent action logs for external audit, regulatory submission, or incident investigation. Export format MUST be machine-readable (JSONL) and human-readable (Markdown). Satisfies OMB M-24-10 (AI use case inventories) and EU AI Act post-market monitoring.

data.title: Human Escalation Threshold
data.description: specsmith MUST provide a configurable human-escalation threshold. When preflight confidence is below the threshold, or when a destructive/release intent is detected, execution MUST pause and route to human review. Satisfies NIST AI RMF (GO-4), OMB M-24-10 human-in-the-loop requirements.

data.title: Emergency Kill-Switch and Circuit-Breaker
data.description: specsmith MUST implement an emergency kill-switch that immediately halts agent activity when: (a) the user invokes a stop command, (b) an anomaly detector triggers, or (c) the retry budget is exhausted. The kill-switch MUST be reachable from both the CLI and the REST API. Satisfies EU AI Act Art. 14 (human oversight), NIST AI RMF.

data.title: Post-Market Behavioral Monitoring and Alerting
data.description: specsmith MUST monitor agent behavior across sessions and alert when anomalous patterns are detected: unexpected confidence regression, unusual tool usage rates, or systematic requirement mismatches. Satisfies EU AI Act Art. 72 (post-market monitoring) and OMB M-24-10.

data.title: One-Click Rollback for File-Modifying Actions
data.description: Every agent action that modifies governance files MUST create a timestamped backup before overwriting, and MUST expose a rollback command that restores the pre-action state. Satisfies NIST AI GenAI Profile (rollback and compensation), CFPB.

records.label: Safe Append-Only Write for New Governance Entries

data.title: Safe Append-Only Write for New Governance Entries
data.description: All new entries appended to governance files (LEDGER.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, TESTS.md) MUST use append-only mode — never truncating or overwriting existing content. Protects against data loss from agent errors. Satisfies EU AI Act technical controls and data integrity requirements.

records.label: AI Disclosure Metadata in Agent Outputs

data.title: AI Disclosure Metadata in Agent Outputs
data.description: Outputs generated by specsmith agents MUST include AI disclosure metadata when surfaced to end-users: which model was used, provider, confidence level, and whether the output was governance-gated. Satisfies FTC Operation AI Comply, Utah SB149 (2024) disclosure requirements.

data.title: Regulatory Compliance Export Report
data.description: specsmith MUST generate a structured compliance export report covering: AI system inventory, risk classification, human oversight controls, audit log summary, and incident history. Satisfies OMB M-24-10, Colorado SB24-205 impact assessments, EU AI Act technical docs.

data.title: Agent Risk Classification Before Deployment
data.description: Before activating a governance profile or agent session, specsmith MUST classify the intended use case against EU AI Act risk tiers (prohibited, high-risk Annex III, GPAI systemic-risk, or minimal-risk). High-risk use cases MUST require additional confirmation. Satisfies EU AI Act Art. 6 risk-based approach.

data.title: Least-Privilege Agent Permissions
data.description: Every agent session MUST operate with the minimum permissions required. Agent capabilities MUST be explicitly declared, and sensitive operations (commit, push, create PR, external calls) MUST be individually gated. Satisfies EU AI Act agent registration, NIST AI RMF least-privilege principle.

data.title: Self-Optimization Bounded by Iteration Budget
data.description: All self-improvement loops (agent improve, eval harness, continuous learning) MUST be bounded by a configurable iteration budget. Unbounded recursion or runaway optimisation MUST be prevented. Satisfies EU AI Act systemic risk controls (GPAISR) and credit spend governance.

records.label: Local-First Model Routing for Governance Tasks

data.title: Local-First Model Routing for Governance Tasks
data.description: Governance classification tasks (preflight, verify, classify_intent) MUST default to a local Ollama model (zero cloud cost) and only escalate to cloud APIs when local confidence is below threshold. Reduces credit spend and avoids unnecessary data transfer to third parties.

records.label: Policy Guardrails at the Interface Layer

data.title: Policy Guardrails at the Interface Layer
data.description: specsmith MUST enforce allow/deny lists for tool categories and data access patterns at the agent interface layer — not just within prompts. Agents MUST NOT access live production databases or unmanaged data sources. Satisfies EU AI Act technical controls, California ADMT data governance requirements.

data.title: GPU-Aware Context Window Sizing
data.description: Before starting an Ollama agent session, specsmith MUST detect available GPU VRAM (NVIDIA via nvidia-smi, AMD via rocm-smi) and recommend a num_ctx value. VRAM tiers: <6 GB → 4096, 6-12 GB → 8192, 12-20 GB → 16384, >=20 GB → 32768. CPU-only defaults to 4096. The function MUST never raise on any platform.

data.title: Live Context Fill Indicator
data.description: Every active agent conversation MUST track and emit context fill events with schema: {type: context_fill, used: int, limit: int, pct: float}. The fill percentage MUST be surfaced in the terminal UI as a compact progress bar (green 0-60%, yellow 60-80%, orange 80-90%, red >90%).

records.label: Auto Context Compression at Configurable Threshold

data.title: Auto Context Compression at Configurable Threshold
data.description: When context fill reaches the configurable compression threshold (default 80%, range 50-95%), specsmith MUST automatically trigger context summarization. Compression MUST emit a context_compressed event with before/after token counts. Auto-compression MUST be togglable; when off, only a warning is surfaced.

data.title: Hard Context Reservation — Never 100% Fill
data.description: The context window MUST NEVER be allowed to reach 100% fill. A hard reservation of 15% (or MIN_FREE_TOKENS=2048, whichever is more restrictive) MUST remain free. When fill reaches the hard ceiling (default 85%), ContextFullError MUST be raised and emergency compression triggered regardless of the auto-compress toggle.

data.title: Dev/Stable Update Channel Persistence
data.description: specsmith MUST persist a user-chosen update channel (stable or dev) to ~/.specsmith/channel. specsmith channel set {stable|dev} writes the file; channel clear removes it. effective_channel_with_source() MUST return (channel, source) where source is user when the file exists, otherwise

data.title: ESDB JSON Export Command
data.description: specsmith esdb export [--output PATH] [--json] MUST dump all ESDB records (requirements and testcases) to a versioned JSON payload at the specified path, or default to <project>/.specsmith/esdb_export.json. Output includes esdb_version,  ackend,

data.title: ESDB JSON Import Command
data.description: specsmith esdb import <source> [--json] MUST validate a JSON export file (checking for

data.title: ESDB Timestamped Backup Command
data.description: specsmith esdb backup [--dir DIR] [--json] MUST create a timestamped snapshot at <dir>/esdb_backup_<YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ>.json (default dir: .specsmith/backups/). The snapshot payload MUST include esdb_version, 	imestamp,  ackend,

data.title: ESDB WAL Rollback Command
data.description: specsmith esdb rollback [--steps N] [--json] MUST report the number of WAL events that would be undone. In stub mode (ChronoMemory native engine not linked) it MUST return {ok: true, steps_requested: N, records_before: N, note: "..."} without modifying state.

data.title: ESDB WAL Compact Command
data.description: specsmith esdb compact [--json] MUST request WAL compaction. In stub mode it MUST return {ok: true, backend: "...", records: N, note: "..."} without error.

data.title: Skills Deactivate Command
data.description: specsmith skills deactivate <skill-id> [--project-dir DIR] MUST set ctive: false in the skill's skill.json, return True on success, and exit non-zero with an error message if the skill is not found.

data.title: Skills Delete Command
data.description: specsmith skills delete <skill-id> [--project-dir DIR] [--yes] MUST prompt for confirmation unless --yes is provided, then permanently remove the skill directory under .specsmith/skills/. Returns non-zero if the skill is not found.

data.title: MCP Server Config Generation Command
data.description: specsmith mcp generate <description> [--json] MUST produce a deterministic MCP server configuration stub with id,

data.title: Agent Ask Keyword Dispatcher
data.description: specsmith agent ask <prompt> [--project-dir DIR] [--json-output] MUST route prompts to the appropriate subsystem by keyword matching (compliance, audit, skill, esdb, mcp, session) without requiring an LLM. It MUST return {reply, action, prompt} and print human-readable output unless --json-output is set.

data.title: Kairos ESDB Settings Page
data.description: The Kairos settings sidebar MUST include an ESDB page under the Specsmith umbrella group. The page MUST display current ESDB status (record count, backend, chain validity) and provide action buttons for Refresh, Export JSON, Import, Backup, Rollback, and Compact.

data.title: Kairos Skills Settings Page
data.description: The Kairos settings sidebar MUST include a Skills page under the Specsmith umbrella group. The page MUST display a description of the Skills system and instructions for using specsmith skills build and related commands.

data.title: Kairos Eval Settings Page
data.description: The Kairos settings sidebar MUST include an Eval page under the Specsmith umbrella group. The page MUST describe the evaluation tracking system and direct users to specsmith eval run for generating reports.

records.label: Kairos AI Providers Table Without Column Overflow

data.title: Kairos AI Providers Table Without Column Overflow
data.description: The Kairos Agents > Providers settings page MUST display AI models in a table with fixed-width columns (Name: 200px, Model ID: 220px, Context: 80px, Output: 80px) using ConstrainedBox + Clipped elements. Long model names such as o4-mini-deep-research MUST NOT overflow into adjacent columns.

data.title: Kairos MCP AI Builder Card
data.description: The Kairos Agents > MCP servers list page MUST include a collapsible AI Builder card that accepts a natural-language server description, calls specsmith mcp generate <description> --json, displays the generated JSON stub, and offers an 'Add to ~/.specsmith/mcp.json' button that appends the stub to the user's MCP config file.

data.title: HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard Sync
data.description: specsmith MUST implement `src/specsmith/agent/hf_leaderboard.py` that fetches model benchmark data from the HuggingFace Datasets Server (`datasets-server.huggingface.co/rows?dataset=open-llm-leaderboard/contents`). The sync MUST be paginated (100 rows/page) and persist results to `~/.specsmith/model_scores.json` under a `bucket_scores` key.

data.title: HF Leaderboard Rate-Limit Handling
data.description: The HF leaderboard sync MUST handle HTTP 429 with exponential-backoff retry (up to 4 attempts). It MUST parse the `RateLimit: "api";r=X;t=Y` header to extract the exact reset window and wait accordingly. A +1 s safety margin MUST be added to the `t=` value.

data.title: HF API Token Support
data.description: When `SPECSMITH_HF_TOKEN` or `hf_api_token` is configured, the HF sync MUST include an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. The CLI `specsmith model-intel test-hf` MUST validate the token via `huggingface.co/api/whoami-v2` and report whether the Datasets Server is reachable.

data.title: HF Leaderboard Static Fallback
data.description: When HF is unreachable (network error, 5xx, or zero parseable rows), specsmith MUST load built-in static benchmark scores covering at least 40 models (OpenAI GPT-4o/mini, Claude 3.5 sonnet/haiku, Gemini 2.x, Mistral, Qwen, Llama, DeepSeek, Phi). The fallback MUST be transparent to callers.

data.title: Bucket Scoring Engine
data.description: specsmith MUST compute three task-bucket scores from raw benchmark values (0–100 scale): Reasoning = 0.35×MATH + 0.30×GPQA + 0.25×BBH + 0.10×IFEval; Conversational = 0.40×IFEval + 0.35×MMLU-PRO + 0.25×BBH; Longform = 0.35×MUSR + 0.35×IFEval + 0.30×MMLU-PRO. Scores MUST be rounded to 2 decimal places.

data.title: Model Intelligence Recommendations
data.description: `specsmith model-intel recommendations [--bucket reasoning|conversational|longform]` MUST return the top-10 models sorted by the requested bucket score. The governance HTTP server MUST expose `GET /api/model-intel/recommendations?bucket=<name>` returning the same data.

data.title: Model Intelligence CLI Commands
data.description: specsmith MUST provide a `model-intel` CLI group with subcommands: `sync` (run HF sync), `scores [--model NAME]` (list/get cached scores), `recommendations [--bucket NAME]` (top-10 per bucket), `test-hf` (connectivity probe). All commands MUST support `--json` flag.

data.title: Model Capability Profiles
data.description: specsmith MUST implement `src/specsmith/agent/model_profiles.py` with a `ModelProfile` TypedDict containing `max_tokens`, `temperature`, `ctx_budget`, `action_capable`, `prompt_style` fields. A `get_profile(model)` function MUST resolve by prefix matching (longest key first) over ≥40 known models.

data.title: Context History Trimmer
data.description: `trim_history(messages, budget_chars)` in `model_profiles.py` MUST trim conversation history to fit within `budget_chars`. Oldest turns MUST be summarised into a compact `[Earlier conversation summary — N turns condensed]` assistant message rather than silently dropped. System messages MUST always be preserved.

data.title: AI Model Pacer EMA Utilisation
data.description: The `ModelRateLimitScheduler` MUST track RPM and TPM utilisation as exponentially-weighted moving averages (alpha=0.25) and expose them in `snapshot()` as `rpm_ema` and `tpm_ema` fields.

data.title: AI Model Pacer Adaptive Concurrency
data.description: `on_rate_limit(model, error, attempt)` MUST decrease `dynamic_concurrency` by 1 (minimum=1) and set `reduced_until` to now+120 s. Concurrency MUST restore incrementally (1 step per 60 s) once `reduced_until` has passed. The method MUST return a float delay for the caller to sleep.

records.label: AI Model Pacer Image Token Estimation

data.title: AI Model Pacer Image Token Estimation
data.description: `estimate_request_tokens()` MUST accept an `image_count` parameter and include `image_count × image_token_estimate` tokens in the reservation. The default `image_token_estimate` MUST be 4096.

data.title: Multi-Provider LLM Client with Fallback
data.description: specsmith MUST implement `src/specsmith/agent/llm_client.py` with a `LLMProvider` ABC and `LLMClient` that tries providers in order, falling back on HTTP 401/403/429/5xx. Concrete providers MUST cover Mistral, OpenAI, Google Gemini, and Ollama. A `MockProvider` MUST be available for tests.

data.title: LLM Client O-Series Translation
data.description: When the model name starts with `o1`, `o3`, or `o4`, or contains `-o1-`/`-o3-`/`-o4-`, the LLM client MUST use `max_completion_tokens` instead of `max_tokens`, force temperature to 1, and rename `system` role messages to `developer`.

data.title: LLM Client vLLM Guided-JSON Mode
data.description: When a JSON schema is provided and the provider type is `byoe` or `huggingface`, the request MUST include `guided_json` and `chat_template_kwargs: {"enable_thinking": false}` to suppress chain-of-thought tokens and enforce structured output.

data.title: Endpoint Preset Registry
data.description: `src/specsmith/agent/provider_registry.py` MUST export `ENDPOINT_PRESETS` — a list of built-in connection presets for at least: vLLM (localhost:8000), LM Studio (localhost:1234), llama.cpp (localhost:8080), OpenRouter, Together AI, Groq, Fireworks, DeepInfra, Perplexity, and Azure OpenAI. Each preset MUST include `id`, `label`, `base_url`, `endpoint_kind`, and `needs_key`.

data.title: Endpoint Probe Enriched Metadata
data.description: `probe_openai_compatible()` MUST return a `models_detail` list where each entry includes `id`, `owner`, `context_length` (from `max_model_len` on vLLM, `context_length` or `context_window` otherwise), and `description`. The cap MUST be 200 models.

data.title: Suggested Profile Generation
data.description: `specsmith agent suggest-profiles` MUST inspect available backends (cloud env vars, installed Ollama models, saved BYOE endpoints) and propose ready-to-add `ProviderEntry` suggestions with role-tuned temperature and max_tokens for the reasoning/conversational/longform AEE buckets. Suggestions MUST be inert (not auto-saved).

records.label: Kairos AI Settings Bucket Score Display

data.title: Kairos AI Settings Bucket Score Display
data.description: The Kairos Agents > Providers settings page MUST display bucket scores (reasoning, conversational, longform) retrieved from `GET /api/model-intel/scores/{model}` for each configured provider. Scores MUST be shown as compact numeric badges. A Sync button MUST call `POST /api/model-intel/sync`.

data.title: YAML-First Governance Sync Pipeline
data.description: When `.specsmith/governance-mode` contains `yaml`, `specsmith sync` MUST read docs/requirements/*.yml and docs/tests/*.yml as canonical sources, write .specsmith/requirements.json + testcases.json as JSON caches, and regenerate docs/REQUIREMENTS.md + docs/TESTS.md as derived artifacts. Legacy Markdown mode (governance-mode absent or `markdown`) MUST still work for backward compatibility.

data.title: Strict Governance Schema Validation
data.description: `specsmith validate --strict` MUST enforce 8 governance schema checks: (1) duplicate REQ IDs, (2) duplicate TEST IDs, (3) missing required REQ fields (id/title/status), (4) missing required TEST fields (id/title/requirement_id), (5) orphaned TESTs (reference non-existent REQ), (6) untested REQs (warning), (7) duplicate REQ titles (warning), (8) machine-state drift between YAML and JSON (warning). Exits 1 on errors; warnings do not block. `--json` flag emits structured output.

records.label: Generate Docs Command Renders YAML to Markdown

data.title: Generate Docs Command Renders YAML to Markdown
data.description: `specsmith generate docs` MUST read docs/requirements/*.yml and docs/tests/*.yml in YAML-first mode, render the canonical Markdown artifacts docs/REQUIREMENTS.md and docs/TESTS.md, and also re-sync the JSON machine state. `--check` flag MUST report what would change without writing. Only available when governance-mode is `yaml`.

records.label: Governance Mode Flag Controls Authority Direction

data.title: Governance Mode Flag Controls Authority Direction
data.description: `.specsmith/governance-mode` MUST contain `yaml` to activate YAML-first mode. `is_yaml_mode(root)` in `specsmith.governance_yaml` reads this flag. Absence of the file or value `markdown` activates legacy Markdown-primary mode. The flag is written by `scripts/migrate_governance_to_yaml.py`.

data.title: YAML Governance Migration Script
data.description: `scripts/migrate_governance_to_yaml.py` MUST be idempotent and execute the following steps in order: (1) remove duplicate REQs from REQUIREMENTS.md, (2) re-sync .specsmith/ JSON from cleaned MD, (3) export JSON to grouped YAML files under docs/requirements/ and docs/tests/, (4) write .specsmith/governance-mode = yaml. Re-running must not corrupt the governance state.
